fix: 1.7B model quality and performance improvements (#30)

* fix: variant-specific model file lists for 1.7B download

The 1.7B HuggingFace repo has different ONNX data files than 0.6B:
- 0.6B has vocoder.onnx.data but no code_predictor.onnx.data
- 1.7B has code_predictor.onnx.data but no vocoder.onnx.data

Split ExpectedFiles into shared base + variant-specific extras:
- Extra06BFiles: vocoder.onnx.data
- Extra17BFiles: code_predictor.onnx.data + CP projection weights

Also adds Issue30_17BNoiseTest diagnostic console app for manual
validation of 1.7B vs 0.6B audio output quality.

Fixes #30

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Allows skipping 0.6B or 1.7B test sections independently.
Saves time when re-running only the 1.7B tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: 1.7B inference bugs — speaker embedding overflow, mask buffer, vocoder validation

- EmbeddingStore.GetSpeakerEmbedding: use _hiddenSize instead of hardcoded
  1024 so 1.7B (hidden=2048) returns full-dimension embeddings for speaker
  similarity search
- LanguageModel.BuildPrefillEmbedding: fix IndexOutOfRangeException when
  injecting 1024-dim speaker embedding into 2048-dim talker space on 1.7B
  by zero-padding the upper dimensions
- LanguageModel: size attention mask buffer to prefillLen + maxNewTokens + 1
  instead of hardcoded 2048 to prevent overflow on long sequences
- Vocoder.Decode: add output validation that throws InvalidOperationException
  when vocoder output samples != timesteps * 1920, providing a clear error
  message pointing to the ONNX model's fixed time dimension as root cause

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add 1.7B vocoder re-export guide for issue #30

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(1.7B): re-export vocoder with dynamic shapes and update download list

- Fix export_vocoder.py: add VocoderOnnxWrapper to bypass transformers 5.5
  create_causal_mask tracing issues, patch BigVGAN _get_extra_padding_for_conv1d
  to prevent baked constants, add sliding window causal mask
- Fix ModelDownloader.cs: add vocoder.onnx.data to Extra17BFiles array
  (1.7B vocoder needs split external data just like 0.6B)
- Update validate_vocoder.py for dynamic shape validation
- Add python/onnx_1.7b/ to .gitignore (generated files for HuggingFace upload)

The 1.7B vocoder on HuggingFace had a fixed T=10 time dimension, producing
only 0.80s of audio regardless of input length. Re-exporting with the wrapper
approach produces correct variable-length output (T x 1920 samples).

Validated: T=5,10,20,50,100,200 all produce correct sample counts.
246 tests pass (236 Core + 10 VoiceCloning).

Fixes #30 (partial - vocoder files need HuggingFace upload)

- vocoder.onnx.data is included in 1.7B expected files

- 1.7B has 4 additional files beyond 0.6B (vocoder data, CP data, projection weights)

* fix: re-export vocoder ONNX with dynamic shapes and correct RoPE inv_freq

Three bugs fixed in the vocoder ONNX export:

1. Fixed time dimension baked as constant: Created VocoderOnnxWrapper that
   bypasses create_causal_mask and builds attention masks/position_ids from
   tensor ops (ONNX-traceable Shape->Range->comparisons).

2. Fixed do_constant_folding corrupting model: Set do_constant_folding=False
   to prevent incorrect pre-computation of attention intermediates.

3. Fixed RoPE inv_freq non-persistent buffer: transformers from_pretrained
   uses device=meta during init, leaving inv_freq (persistent=False) as
   uninitialized garbage. Added _fix_rope_inv_freq() to explicitly compute
   correct values before export and validation.

Also added 7 compatibility patches for transformers 5.5.0 + qwen_tts 0.1.1.

Validation results (cross-process):

  T=10: max_err=5.07e-06 PASS
  T=50: max_err=1.37e-04 PASS
  T=100: max_err=1.32e-04 PASS

feat: add App.get_app_graph() and App._get_graph_token() (#355)

`App` had no way to access a Graph client for app-only operations — only
`ActivityContext` did.

## Changes

- **`App._get_graph_token(tenant_id=None)`** — new private method
wrapping `_token_manager.get_graph_token()`, mirroring the existing
`_get_bot_token` pattern.

- **`App.get_app_graph(tenant_id=None)`** — new public method returning
a `GraphServiceClient` for app-only Graph operations. Not cached
(clients own caching if needed). Accepts optional `tenant_id` for
multi-tenant support.

- **`utils/graph.py`** — new utility module with a public
`create_graph_client` helper (replaces the private `_get_graph_client`
inline function in `activity_context.py`). Both `ActivityContext` and
`App` now import it from `utils`.

- **`ActivityContext.app_graph`** — unchanged.

- **`ActivityProcessor`** — unchanged.

```python
# App-level Graph access, no ActivityContext needed
graph = app.get_app_graph()                         # default tenant
graph = app.get_app_graph(tenant_id="some-tenant")  # explicit tenant
me = await graph.me.get()
```

* fix: check trait default method bodies for assign_target_kinds

Type checker skipped Item::Trait in check_item, leaving assign_target_kinds
unpopulated for assignments inside trait default method bodies.  MLIR codegen
uses requireAssignTargetKindOf (fail-closed) for every assignment, so the
missing entry caused a hard error on the bump() default in
param_mutability_codegen.hew.

Fix: add check_trait_defaults, which constructs a synthetic FnDecl for each
default method body and runs check_function_as on it.  This binds params into
env so Expr::Identifier targets resolve to LocalVar and get an entry in the
assign_target_kinds side-table.

Also add assign_target_kinds as the 7th required field in
packWithSchema / drop_funcs_roundtrip / method_call_receiver_kinds_roundtrip
in test_msgpack_reader.cpp; parseMsgpackAST uses mapReq so any minimal-valid
program fixture must include it.

watchdog: fix fractional timestamp parsing in daemon_recently_active()

journalctl --output=short-unix produces timestamps like 1234567890.123456
on some systemd versions. The regex ^[0-9]+\$ rejects the decimal point,
causing daemon_recently_active() to always return false. The busy-streak
guard (rpc_busy_streak) therefore never fires and every RPC timeout goes
straight to fail_streak, triggering unnecessary restarts of a live node.

Fix: strip fractional seconds with \ before the regex check.

fix: redirect stdin for sg docker calls in SSH heredocs

sg docker spawns a subshell that inherits stdin from the SSH
heredoc pipe. This causes it to consume the remaining script
content, preventing subsequent commands (like the actual migration)
from executing. Adding </dev/null to all sg docker calls ensures
the heredoc stdin is preserved for the remote shell.

This was the root cause of the login failure — migrations never
ran, so the users table did not exist in the database.

commit.message:
fix: auto-retry with resumeSession when agent evicts stale cached session

When the Copilot agent subprocess drops a session from its internal map
(due to inactivity/memory pressure), our cached CopilotSession object
becomes stale. Previously this caused permanent "Session not found"
errors until server restart.

Now _doWork() catches "Session not found" from session.send(), evicts
the stale cache entry, calls resumeSession() to re-register with the
agent, and retries the send — self-healing without restart.

commit.message:
r600: add Terakan Shader ABI Contract (Phase 3 design)

Formal hardware resource allocation map for TeraScale-2 Vulkan driver,
validated by two independent rubber-duck reviews:

- KCACHE bank map: 0-13 UBOs, 14 robustness metadata, 15 push constants
- Write robustness via dedicated bank 14 buffer (not push constants)
  avoids ABI break, cache invalidation, and bank 15 indexing restriction
- Read robustness is zero-cost (hardware-enforced by VTX/TEX units)
- Saturating bounds check guards ALL MEM_RAT ops (stores + atomics)
- 4-phase pass discipline: resource-agnostic → layout → ABI → cleanup
- System value 3-tier folding: compile-time → layout-derived → runtime
- Complete resource/sampler/UAV slot budgets from header analysis

Findings from EVERGREEN_OOB_ROBUSTNESS_FINDINGS.md (Phase 5 probes)
drive the read/write asymmetry that makes this architecture viable.

commit.message:
Adding "winapp ui" for UI automation (#380)

## Description

Adds a new winapp ui command group that enables UI automation for
Windows apps using Microsoft UI Automation (UIA). This allows
developers and AI agents to programmatically discover, inspect, and
interact with UI elements across any Windows application.

## Subcommands

| Command | Description |
| ---------------- |
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
|
| ui list-windows | List visible windows for an app |
| ui status | Connect to an app and print session info |
| ui inspect | Dump the UI tree (or ancestors/subtree) |
| ui search | Find elements by text query |
| ui invoke | Activate an element via UIA patterns (Invoke, Toggle,
Select, ExpandCollapse) |
| ui click | Click via mouse input at screen coordinates (fallback for
non-invocable controls) |
| ui focus | Move keyboard focus to an element |
| ui set-value | Set text/value on editable controls |
| ui get-property | Read UIA property values from an element |
| ui get-focused | Report the currently focused element |
| ui screenshot | Save a PNG of a window or element |
| ui scroll | Scroll a container (up/down/left/right/top/bottom) |
| ui scroll-into-view | Scroll a target element into view |
| ui wait-for | Wait for element appearance, disappearance, or property
change |

## Key design decisions

- Semantic slug selectors — inspect and search output stable, shell-safe
element slugs (e.g., btn-close-d1a2) that can be passed
 directly to other commands
- Flexible app targeting — Target apps by process name, window title,
PID, or HWND (--window for stability)
- Invokable ancestor fallback — search and invoke can surface/use a
parent element that supports invoke when the matched child
does not
- Mouse input fallback — click uses real mouse injection (SetCursorPos +
SendInput) for controls that don't expose UIA invoke
patterns
- Testable architecture — UIA and session services are behind interfaces
with fakes for unit testing

## Example usage

 winapp ui inspect --app notepad
 winapp ui search "Save" --app myapp
 winapp ui invoke btn-save-d1a2 --app myapp
 winapp ui screenshot --app myapp --output screen.png
 winapp ui wait-for btn-done-f3c1 --app myapp --timeout 10

## Type of Change

Add locks to buildtrees and packages. (#1979)

This is a followup on https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg-tool/pull/1938
Resolves
https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg/pull/51004#issuecomment-4195577101

@autoantwort points out that our MSBuild integration leaves the default
buildtrees location and only overrides the installed root:


https://github.com/microsoft/vcpkg/blob/81e3c888ef717a026eb57bb2f3cb0dbd30246271/scripts/buildsystems/msbuild/vcpkg.targets#L183-L190

I initially tried a solution which would get `buildtrees()` out of
`VcpkgPaths` entirely and pass around something that had both the data
and effectively encapsulated that things had been locked, but changes to
make that happen were starting to get huge, and I saw that I had already
accidentally created deadlocks by being inconsistent about the order of
taking the lock on the installed tree vs. buildtrees and packages, so I
gave up on it. (
https://github.com/BillyONeal/vcpkg-tool/commit/0a4b9ef757f68fd784bc14994bb7fb85ecd182eb

commit.message:
Add 'winapp new' command for creating WinUI 3 projects and items (#411)

## Summary

Adds a new top-level \winapp new\ command that scaffolds WinUI 3
projects and items using the
\Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK.WinUI.CSharp.Templates\ package.

## What it does

- **Interactive mode**: Presents a template picker and name prompt when
run without arguments
- **Non-interactive mode**: \winapp new winui -n MyApp\ creates a
project with defaults
- **Context-aware**: Outside a \.csproj\ directory shows project
templates (winui, winui-navview, etc.); inside one shows item templates
(winui-page, winui-window, etc.)
- **Auto-installs/updates** the template package on each invocation
- **Dynamic discovery**: Reads template.json from the nupkg — no
hardcoded template names or parameters
- **Post-creation setup**: Configures RuntimeIdentifier for current arch
and adds the WinApp build tools NuGet package
- **Next steps**: Shows \cd <name>\ + \dotnet run\ after project
creation
- **Unique default names**: Avoids collisions with existing directories
(\winui-app\, \winui-app2\, etc.)

## Architecture

Introduces an \ITemplateProvider\ abstraction for future C++ template
support:

\\\
ITemplateProvider (per-language contract)
  └── DotNetTemplateProvider  (C# via dotnet new)
  └── [future] CppTemplateProvider

ITemplateService (composite, aggregates all providers)
  └── NewCommand talks to this
\\\

Adding a new language later is a single DI registration line.

## Usage examples

\\\ ash
# Interactive
winapp new

# Create a blank WinUI app
winapp new winui

# Create a NavigationView app with a name
winapp new winui-navview -n MyApp

# Add a page to an existing project (from inside the project dir)
winapp new winui-page -n SettingsPage

# Pass extra dotnet new arguments
winapp new winui -n MyApp -- --dotnet-version net9.0
\\\

## Files

### New (7 files)
- \Commands/NewCommand.cs\ — Command with interactive + non-interactive
flows
- \Services/ITemplateProvider.cs\ — Per-language provider interface
- \Services/DotNetTemplateProvider.cs\ — C# provider (reads nupkg, wraps
dotnet new)
- \Services/ITemplateService.cs\ — Composite service interface
- \Services/TemplateService.cs\ — Aggregates all providers
- \Models/TemplateInfo.cs\ — Template/parameter models + JSON source-gen
- \WinApp.Cli.Tests/NewCommandTests.cs\ — 15 tests

### Modified
- \WinAppRootCommand.cs\, \HostBuilderExtensions.cs\ — DI registration
- \README.md\, \docs/usage.md\, \docs/guides/dotnet.md\, setup skill —

commit.message:
test(parser): add regression tests for sorted-sessions cache plan.md guard (#861)

Add two tests to TestSortedSessionsCacheSkipsRedundantSort:

- test_sort_runs_after_plan_change: verifies that adding plan.md to a
  session (while events.jsonl is unchanged) forces a re-sort via the
  `not deferred_sessions` guard, and the returned summary carries the
  fresh session name.

- test_sort_skipped_after_plan_change_then_unchanged: verifies that
  after the plan.md change settles, a subsequent call with no further
  changes hits the warm cache and skips the sort.

commit.message:
✨ Add framework matrix workflow generation

Create FrameworkWorkflowGenerator that produces GHA workflow YAML with
ruby × framework-version matrix strategy from .kettle-jem.yml config.

- framework-ci.yml.example template with placeholder matrix
- Generator populates matrix include entries from config versions
- Supports dot-separated (ar-7.0.x) and underscore (rails_7_0) patterns
- GithubWorkflows phase conditionally generates when preset=framework
- SmartMerger preserves destination customizations on subsequent runs
- 7 new specs covering both ActiveRecord and Rails patterns
